raw_body:
Anthropic launched a beta version of its Claude Tag feature for Enterprise and Team tiers, shifting its chat model into shared Slack channels. Moving away from traditional isolated chat boxes, users pull the artificial intelligence model into active group threads by typing @Claude.  The integration allows any team member in the channel to delegate a task, review […]
The post Anthropic drops ‘workplace AI agents’ directly inside Slack appeared first on AI News.
speedrun: Anthropic has introduced a beta feature called Claude Tag, integrating its AI directly into Slack for Enterprise and Team users. This allows team members to engage the AI in active group conversations by simply typing @Claude. This shift from isolated chat boxes to collaborative threads could enhance team productivity as members can delegate tasks and get real-time assistance. This matters now as remote work continues to rise, making effective collaboration tools essential.
why_it_matters: Teams can streamline communication and task management by directly involving AI in discussions, enhancing efficiency.
This move reflects a broader trend of integrating AI into collaborative platforms, indicating a shift towards more interactive workplace tools.

lenses.eli12: Anthropic's new feature lets you bring AI into your Slack conversations with a simple command. It’s like having a helpful assistant right in the middle of your team chats. This could make it easier for everyone to work together and get things done faster, which is important as more people work remotely.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, the Claude Tag feature addresses the need for seamless collaboration within teams. By embedding AI in Slack, it could reduce the time spent on task delegation and improve overall efficiency. This integration might inspire new features or tools that enhance team dynamics and productivity.
lenses.engineer: The Claude Tag feature integrates Anthropic's AI model directly into Slack channels, allowing real-time interaction by mentioning @Claude. This approach contrasts with traditional isolated AI chat interfaces, potentially improving user engagement and task management. However, specific performance benchmarks or model comparisons were not provided, so the impact on system load or response times remains to be seen.
